etymology.description: Phonetic 我 (wǒ, "I") + semantic 羊 (yáng, "good; beautiful; auspicious") = association with virtue -> righteousness
etymology.interpretation: The character 義 (yì, "righteousness") combines the notion of self from 我 (wǒ, "I") with the auspicious connotations of 羊 (yáng, "good") to express the idea of personal integrity and virtue.
etymology.simplification: 義(義) was simplified to 义 by removing the phonetic component 我 (wǒ, "I") and retaining only 羊 (yáng, "good") for ease and speed of writing.

examples.chinese: 为了正义(為了正義)，他愿意牺牲自己(他願意犧牲自己)。
examples.pinyin: Wèile zhèngyì, tā yuànyì xīshēng zìjǐ.
examples.english: For justice, he is willing to sacrifice himself.

examples.chinese: 朋友之间的友情应该以义为重(朋友之間的友情應該以義為重)。
examples.pinyin: Péngyǒu zhījiān de yǒuqíng yīnggāi yǐ yì wéi zhòng.
examples.english: The friendship between friends should emphasize righteousness.

examples.chinese: 古代的英雄常被赞为有勇有义(古代的英雄常被讚為有勇有義)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài de yīngxióng cháng bèi zàn wéi yǒu yǒng yǒu yì.
examples.english: Ancient heroes are often praised as being courageous and righteous.
